description: tool-calling-tutor skill evals — 5 test cases covering each of the 4 triage branches + a routing-away case

evals.name: symptom_a_llm_not_calling
evals.input: 我寫了一個 tool 叫 get_weather、tools=[get_weather] 也傳進去了、可是 Claude 直接回了一段文字、根本沒呼叫 tool。哪裡錯了？
expected_behavior: Should first ask which of 4 symptoms (a/b/c/d) the user is hitting
After user confirms (a), should reference references/debug-flowchart.md Section A
Should mention the 5 common causes: description too generic, overlapping boundaries, query doesn't need tool, missing schema wrapper, model too small
Should ask user to inspect description before guessing

evals.name: symptom_b_wrong_args
evals.input: Tool 有被呼叫但 args 不對。user 說 'Convert 32 Celsius'、結果 value 傳成 '32 Celsius' 而不是 32。
expected_behavior: Should suggest 3 fixes: change type to number, add required, add enum for unit
Should NOT just write a fixed schema for the user — should walk them through the 4 iterations

evals.name: symptom_c_loop_no_end
evals.input: ReAct loop 跑滿 max_iter=10 都沒收尾、LLM 一直叫同一個 tool。
expected_behavior: Should check: (1) is assistant response appended to messages? (2) does tool message have tool_call_id? (3) does tool return self-contained result?
Should mention max_iter safety net is mandatory, not optional

evals.name: symptom_d_design_from_scratch
evals.input: 我想做一個 tool 讓 LLM 可以查 GitHub repo 的 star 數、但完全不知道 schema 怎麼寫。
expected_behavior: Should walk through the 5-step recipe (define, describe, type, constrain, error pattern)
Should point user to fork examples/stage-3/02-multi-tool-selection/starter.py as template
Should remind to ask 'which SDK are you using' before showing code

evals.name: route_away_langgraph
evals.input: 我用 LangGraph 寫了一個 agent、StateGraph 的 add_edge 有點問題、能幫我看嗎？
expected_behavior: Should NOT engage with LangGraph-specific debugging
Should route user to Stage 4 — Agent Frameworks
Should mention this skill handles tool-use mechanics, not framework-specific issues
